commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Human review is needed for live Telegram proof or maintainer proof override and compatibility acceptance; there is no narrow automated repair to queue.; Cleared: No new dependency, CI, permission, credential, or supply-chain surface was introduced; the security-sensitive routing change is intended to reduce cross-topic leakage. (sha=f515e5d36ac1166b955838c21489390dbfd9dbe2)
commands.review_summary:
The branch updates Telegram send and draft-stream thread fallback policy, cron isolated-session delivery metadata, heartbeat target reconstruction, and focused tests so explicit topic routes fail closed or remain preserved across async follow-ups.

PR surface: Source +216, Tests +97. Total +313 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level and mocked regression boundaries: the PR comments describe base-fails/head-passes coverage, and the diff adds focused tests for draft, cron, and heartbeat routing. No live Telegram reproduction/proof has been supplied.
commands.review_followup: Human review is needed for live Telegram proof or maintainer proof override and compatibility acceptance; there is no narrow automated repair to queue.

target.title: fix(telegram): preserve forum topic routing
target.body:
## Summary

- Keep Telegram thread-not-found fallback for General topic (`message_thread_id=1`), while failing closed for explicit non-General forum topic sends and direct/private topic sends.
- Preserve Telegram topic routing for automatic async/heartbeat follow-ups by reusing stored topic thread IDs for plugin-owned group routes.
- Seed explicit cron announce targets into isolated-session delivery context so completion and heartbeat notices stay on the configured topic instead of falling back to an agent default group.
- Keep the effective diff focused on Telegram topic routing, cron delivery context, outbound target reconstruction, and the temporary current-main memory-core test-contract shim needed for CI.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] Tests only

## Scope

Telegram extension outbound delivery, shared heartbeat target resolution, isolated cron session delivery metadata, and one test-only `memory-core` mock compatibility update for the current subagent runtime contract.

## Linked Issue / PR

- #78755 independently reported missing `message_thread_id` on outbound Telegram forum sends and was closed as duplicate/superseded by the same topic-routing work.
- #78724 covered the final session auto-reply `message_thread_id` pass-through now present on `main`; this PR keeps the broader fail-closed and async-route preservation behavior.
- Adjacent historical thread/topic work reviewed: #43808, #49704, #52824, #52286, #73900, #12293, #30892.

## Root Cause

The Telegram sender retried `400: message thread not found` by removing `message_thread_id` too broadly. That is safe only for General-topic recovery, but unsafe for explicit non-General forum topics and direct/private topic sends because Telegram can then deliver into the wrong root context.

A separate async path could reconstruct heartbeat/completion delivery from incomplete session metadata. Topic-scoped Telegram sessions and explicit cron announce jobs could lose the thread id before later automatic notices were sent.

## Regression Test Plan

- Explicit forum topic text/media/sticker/poll sends reject after the first thread-not-found error and do not retry threadless.
- Topic-qualified recipient strings such as `telegram:group:<id>:topic:<thread>` fail closed.
- Direct/private and resolved username DM sends with explicit thread ids reject after the first thread-not-found error and do not retry threadless.
- Draft stream preview/materialize sends fail closed for non-General forum topics and keep the existing stricter DM-preview behavior.
- Heartbeat target resolution preserves stored thread IDs for plugin-owned group routes when only `deliveryContext` or `origin.chatType` carries the topic context.
- Explicit Telegram cron announce targets persist session delivery context with the resolved chat id and topic thread id.

## Real Behavior Proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram topic sends must not silently retry without `message_thread_id` after Telegram reports `message thread not found`; async heartbeat/completion delivery must preserve the intended topic route instead of falling back to a group/default route.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout on NixOS Linux after refresh against current `origin/main`; branch `fix/telegram-topic-thread-fallback`; head `75acdc52684b07c8355966394f5ad5347a713e0a`.
- **Exact commands run after this patch**:

```sh
/tmp/openclaw-pr76920-merge/node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 \
  extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.test.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/send.ts \
  src/cron/isolated-agent.direct-delivery-core-channels.test.ts \
  src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts \
  src/infra/outbound/targets.test.ts \
  src/infra/outbound/targets.ts
node scripts/test-projects.mjs \
  extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts \
  src/infra/outbound/targets.test.ts \
  src/cron/isolated-agent.direct-delivery-core-channels.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
```

- **Evidence after fix**: formatter passed; focused Telegram/outbound/cron tests passed 3 Vitest shards / 222 tests; core test typecheck passed; extensions test typecheck passed.
- **Observed result after fix**: explicit non-General forum topic sends and direct/private sends with explicit thread ids now fail closed on `message thread not found` instead of retrying threadless. General-topic fallback remains available, and async Telegram topic route preservation is covered by outbound/cron tests.
- **What was not tested**: no live Telegram Bot API/Desktop proof from a production bot after this update. Mantis visible Telegram proof was requested in the PR comments; maintainers can still require live bot proof or apply an explicit proof override.

## Verification

Current head `75acdc52684b07c8355966394f5ad5347a713e0a` was refreshed against latest `origin/main`, trimmed back to the intended nine-file effective diff, and pushed after the commands above passed locally.

## Security Impact

Reduces cross-topic message leakage in Telegram forum groups and private/direct topic contexts by failing closed when an explicit thread is stale or invalid, and by preserving topic context for automatic follow-up notices.

## Compatibility / Migration

No config migration. Non-General forum topic and direct/private topic sends now surface the Telegram error instead of silently retrying threadless. Explicit cron announce routes now persist the intended delivery context for later automatic notices.

## Risks

If an operator intentionally relied on stale explicit topic sends falling back to a root chat, those sends now fail. That behavior was the leakage bug this PR is meant to prevent.

The cron/session change is intentionally limited to resolved explicit delivery targets; bare `delivery.mode: none` jobs still avoid implicit last-target resolution.

## AI Assistance

AI-assisted refresh, simplification, validation, and PR-body update.
target.author: kesslerio

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

```sh
  src/infra/outbound/targets.test.ts \
  src/cron/isolated-agent.direct-delivery-core-channels.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
```

- **Evidence after fix**: formatter passed; focused Telegram/outbound/cron tests passed 3 Vitest shards / 222 tests; core test typecheck passed; extensions test typecheck passed.
- **Observed result after fix**: explicit non-General forum topic sends and direct/private sends with explicit thread ids now fail closed on `message thread not found` instead of retrying threadless. General-topic fallback remains available, and async Telegram topic route preservation is covered by outbound/cron tests.
- **What was not tested**: no live Telegram Bot API/Desktop proof from a production bot after this update. Mantis visible Telegram proof was requested in the PR comments; maintainers can still require live bot proof or apply an explicit proof override.

## Verification

Current head `75acdc52684b07c8355966394f5ad5347a713e0a` was refreshed against latest `origin/main`, trimmed back to the intended nine-file effective diff, and pushed after the commands above passed locally.

## Security Impact

Reduces cross-topic message leakage in Telegram forum groups and private/direct topic contexts by failing closed when an explicit thread is stale or invalid, and by preserving topic context for automatic follow-up notices.

## Compatibility / Migration

No config migration. Non-General forum topic and direct/private topic sends now surface the Telegram error instead of silently retrying threadless. Explicit cron announce routes now persist the intended delivery context for later automatic notices.

## Risks

If an operator intentionally relied on stale explicit topic sends falling back to a root chat, those sends now fail. That behavior was the leakage bug this PR is meant to prevent.

The cron/session change is intentionally limited to resolved explicit delivery targets; bare `delivery.mode: none` jobs still avoid implicit last-target resolution.

## AI Assistance

AI-assisted refresh, simplification, validation, and PR-body update.

commands.author: kesslerio

target.title: fix(gateway): add provider auth prewarm operator controls
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: provider auth prewarm can still be expensive during gateway startup or recovery, and affected operators do not have a supported way to disable or delay it.
- Solution: expose a gateway-level provider auth prewarm policy with config and env controls.
- What changed: added `gateway.providerAuthPrewarm.enabled`, `gateway.providerAuthPrewarm.delayMs`, and `OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDER_AUTH_PREWARM`; startup, reload, logout-triggered rewarm, delayed startup prewarm, and auth-profile-failure rewarm paths now honor the resolved policy.
- Docs: documented the new config keys and env override in `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md`, and updated the generated config baseline hash.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not claim to fix the underlying event-loop starvation in `warmCurrentProviderAuthState`, Discord READY timing, or OpenAI/Codex route fallback behavior.

## Motivation

This PR follows up on the Discord/operator recovery report in #85997. It also relates to the narrower provider-auth startup/prewarm reproduction in #85999.

This proposes an operator safety valve while maintainers evaluate whether an explicit config/env surface is wanted alongside the deeper scheduling/caching fix for provider auth prewarm. It is intentionally not marked as closing either issue because the user-facing reports cover broader startup, channel readiness, and auth-provider routing behavior.

AI-assisted: drafted and implemented with Codex; human review and maintainer product feedback are requested before merge.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Follow-up to #85997.
- Related to #85999.
- This PR does not close either issue; it proposes a scoped mitigation for provider auth prewarm operator control.
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: affected deployments can now disable provider auth prewarm or increase its startup delay without patching OpenClaw internals.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Ubuntu Linux server, using isolated temp `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` and `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` values. The branch is currently rebased on `origin/main`; the startup proof below was captured from this PR branch with the same config/env policy behavior.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Started a real gateway process from this checkout with an isolated temp `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` and `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`.
  - Used `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1`, `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`, and `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_STARTUP_TRACE=1` to keep the run hermetic while still exercising the gateway startup/post-ready path.
  - Ran three redacted startup cases: default config, `gateway.providerAuthPrewarm.enabled: false`, and config-enabled plus `OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDER_AUTH_PREWARM=1`.
- Evidence after fix (redacted terminal/log proof):

Default config still schedules and runs provider auth prewarm:

```text
[gateway] startup trace: sidecars.ready loadedPluginCount=0.0 postReadySidecarCount=1.0
[gateway] ready
[gateway] provider auth state pre-warmed in 75ms eventLoopMax=0.0ms
[proof] default-enabled elapsedMs=16851 port=<redacted> stateDir=<redacted>
```

Config disable skips the provider auth prewarm sidecar:

```text
[gateway] startup trace: sidecars.ready loadedPluginCount=0.0 postReadySidecarCount=0.0
[gateway] ready
[gateway] startup trace: post-ready.maintenance 13.5ms total=5670.5ms eventLoopMax=0.0ms
[proof] config-disabled elapsedMs=10409 port=<redacted> stateDir=<redacted>
```

Env override skips prewarm even when config enables it:

```text
[gateway] startup trace: sidecars.ready loadedPluginCount=0.0 postReadySidecarCount=0.0
[gateway] ready
[gateway] startup trace: post-ready.maintenance 13.3ms total=5482.6ms eventLoopMax=0.0ms
[proof] env-disabled elapsedMs=10152 port=<redacted> stateDir=<redacted>
```

- Targeted verification:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/models-auth-status.test.ts` -> passed during the final rebase cycle.
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts` -> passed during this PR update cycle.
  - `corepack pnpm config:docs:check` -> passed during this PR update cycle.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:core:test` -> passed on the current pushed branch.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` -> passed on the current pushed branch.
  - GitHub PR checks for the current pushed branch completed with no failures and no pending checks.
- Observed result after fix: default behavior remains enabled; config/env disable paths skip startup prewarm; delayed startup prewarm and auth-profile-failure rewarm re-check the current policy before running; reload and logout rewarm paths skip when the same policy disables provider auth prewarm.
- What was not tested: live Discord restart timing, real provider CLI starvation on a constrained VM, and OpenAI Codex OAuth fallback behavior.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): #85999 includes startup prewarm timing logs; #85997 includes redacted Discord/operator-visible errors.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: current main already defers provider auth prewarm, but the exposed prewarm controls are internal test/runtime options rather than an operator-facing config surface.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no schema-covered config/env option to disable or delay provider auth prewarm on deployments where the prewarm path harms startup or channel recovery.
- Contributing context (if known): #85272 narrowed startup ordering by deferring prewarm; #85999 reports that provider auth prewarm can still monopolize startup on a constrained host.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/models-auth-status.test.ts`, `src/config/config-misc.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: disabled prewarm policy skips startup/reload/logout rewarm paths, env override disables config-enabled prewarm, configured delay is honored, and a long-lived provider-auth sidecar re-checks current config before delayed startup or auth-profile-failure rewarm.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the change is policy resolution and call-site gating; these tests cover each touched call path without requiring live providers.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): startup prewarm ordering coverage existed, but not operator-facing config/env policy.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Adds optional gateway config and env controls:

```json
{
  "gateway": {
    "providerAuthPrewarm": {
      "enabled": false,
      "delayMs": 5000
    }
  }
}
```

`OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDER_AUTH_PREWARM=1` disables provider auth prewarm regardless of config.

Defaults remain unchanged: provider auth prewarm stays enabled with the existing startup delay.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[gateway startup/reload/logout] -> [provider auth prewarm always scheduled]

After:
[gateway startup/reload/logout] -> [resolve config/env policy] -> [schedule or skip provider auth prewarm]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Ubuntu Linux server
- Runtime/container: local Node/pnpm checkout
- Model/provider: provider behavior mocked in tests; real gateway startup proof used isolated temp config/state
- Integration/channel (if any): gateway startup/reload/logout paths; no live Discord run
- Relevant config (redacted): synthetic gateway config with `auth.mode: none`, `controlUi.enabled: false`, temporary port, and no real channel/provider credentials

### Steps

1. Start gateway with default synthetic config and startup trace enabled.
2. Start gateway with `gateway.providerAuthPrewarm.enabled: false`.
3. Start gateway with `gateway.providerAuthPrewarm.enabled: true` plus `OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDER_AUTH_PREWARM=1`.
4. Run targeted gateway/config checks, docs check, and test typechecks.

### Expected

- Default policy schedules and runs provider auth prewarm.
- Disabled policy skips provider auth prewarm.
- Env override wins over config-enabled prewarm.
- Configured delay controls startup prewarm delay.
- Long-lived sidecar rewarm paths respect the current config after hot reload.

### Actual

- Matches expected in redacted real gateway startup logs, targeted tests, local typechecks, docs check, and current PR CI.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: real gateway startup proof for default/config-disabled/env-disabled behavior; targeted gateway/config tests; docs baseline check; `tsgo:core:test`; `tsgo:extensions:test`.
- Edge cases checked: env override disabling config-enabled prewarm; invalid schema values rejected; reload/logout rewarm paths honor the same policy; long-lived sidecar skips delayed startup and auth-profile-failure rewarm after current config disables prewarm.
- What you did **not** verify: live Discord startup recovery, real constrained-host prewarm timing, and provider OAuth fallback behavior.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.
- Current next action: maintainer review/product decision on whether this operator-facing config/env surface should be accepted as an opt-in safety valve.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`Yes`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: no migration required; operators may opt into the new config/env controls.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: disabling prewarm may make model/provider auth status colder until first use.
  - Mitigation: default behavior remains enabled; the disable path is explicit opt-in for affected operators.
- Risk: this may be considered a product/config surface decision rather than the preferred root fix.
  - Mitigation: scoped as a mitigation, and does not close the linked issues.

commands.author: kesslerio

target.title: fix(telegram): preserve forum topic routing
target.body:
## Summary

- Keep Telegram thread-not-found fallback for General topic (`message_thread_id=1`), while failing closed for explicit non-General forum topic sends and direct/private topic sends.
- Preserve Telegram topic routing for automatic async/heartbeat follow-ups by reusing stored topic thread IDs for plugin-owned group routes.
- Seed explicit cron announce targets into isolated-session delivery context so completion and heartbeat notices stay on the configured topic instead of falling back to an agent default group.
- Keep the effective diff focused on Telegram topic routing, cron delivery context, outbound target reconstruction, and the temporary current-main memory-core test-contract shim needed for CI.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] Tests only

## Scope

Telegram extension outbound delivery, shared heartbeat target resolution, isolated cron session delivery metadata, and one test-only `memory-core` mock compatibility update for the current subagent runtime contract.

## Linked Issue / PR

- #78755 independently reported missing `message_thread_id` on outbound Telegram forum sends and was closed as duplicate/superseded by the same topic-routing work.
- #78724 covered the final session auto-reply `message_thread_id` pass-through now present on `main`; this PR keeps the broader fail-closed and async-route preservation behavior.
- Adjacent historical thread/topic work reviewed: #43808, #49704, #52824, #52286, #73900, #12293, #30892.

## Root Cause

The Telegram sender retried `400: message thread not found` by removing `message_thread_id` too broadly. That is safe only for General-topic recovery, but unsafe for explicit non-General forum topics and direct/private topic sends because Telegram can then deliver into the wrong root context.

A separate async path could reconstruct heartbeat/completion delivery from incomplete session metadata. Topic-scoped Telegram sessions and explicit cron announce jobs could lose the thread id before later automatic notices were sent.

## Regression Test Plan

- Explicit forum topic text/media/sticker/poll sends reject after the first thread-not-found error and do not retry threadless.
- Topic-qualified recipient strings such as `telegram:group:<id>:topic:<thread>` fail closed.
- Direct/private and resolved username DM sends with explicit thread ids reject after the first thread-not-found error and do not retry threadless.
- Draft stream preview/materialize sends fail closed for non-General forum topics and keep the existing stricter DM-preview behavior.
- Heartbeat target resolution preserves stored thread IDs for plugin-owned group routes when only `deliveryContext` or `origin.chatType` carries the topic context.
- Explicit Telegram cron announce targets persist session delivery context with the resolved chat id and topic thread id.

## Real Behavior Proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram topic sends must not silently retry without `message_thread_id` after Telegram reports `message thread not found`; async heartbeat/completion delivery must preserve the intended topic route instead of falling back to a group/default route.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout on NixOS Linux after refresh against current `origin/main`; branch `fix/telegram-topic-thread-fallback`; head `75acdc52684b07c8355966394f5ad5347a713e0a`.
- **Exact commands run after this patch**:

```sh
/tmp/openclaw-pr76920-merge/node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 \
  extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.test.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/send.ts \
  src/cron/isolated-agent.direct-delivery-core-channels.test.ts \
  src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts \
  src/infra/outbound/targets.test.ts \
  src/infra/outbound/targets.ts
node scripts/test-projects.mjs \
  extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts \
  src/infra/outbound/targets.test.ts \
  src/cron/isolated-agent.direct-delivery-core-channels.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
```

- **Evidence after fix**: formatter passed; focused Telegram/outbound/cron tests passed 3 Vitest shards / 222 tests; core test typecheck passed; extensions test typecheck passed.
- **Observed result after fix**: explicit non-General forum topic sends and direct/private sends with explicit thread ids now fail closed on `message thread not found` instead of retrying threadless. General-topic fallback remains available, and async Telegram topic route preservation is covered by outbound/cron tests.
- **What was not tested**: no live Telegram Bot API/Desktop proof from a production bot after this update. Mantis visible Telegram proof was requested in the PR comments; maintainers can still require live bot proof or apply an explicit proof override.

## Verification

Current head `75acdc52684b07c8355966394f5ad5347a713e0a` was refreshed against latest `origin/main`, trimmed back to the intended nine-file effective diff, and pushed after the commands above passed locally.

## Security Impact

Reduces cross-topic message leakage in Telegram forum groups and private/direct topic contexts by failing closed when an explicit thread is stale or invalid, and by preserving topic context for automatic follow-up notices.

## Compatibility / Migration

No config migration. Non-General forum topic and direct/private topic sends now surface the Telegram error instead of silently retrying threadless. Explicit cron announce routes now persist the intended delivery context for later automatic notices.

## Risks

If an operator intentionally relied on stale explicit topic sends falling back to a root chat, those sends now fail. That behavior was the leakage bug this PR is meant to prevent.

The cron/session change is intentionally limited to resolved explicit delivery targets; bare `delivery.mode: none` jobs still avoid implicit last-target resolution.

## AI Assistance

AI-assisted refresh, simplification, validation, and PR-body update.

```sh

  extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/send.ts \

node scripts/test-projects.mjs \
  extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts \

  src/cron/isolated-agent.direct-delivery-core-channels.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
```

- **Evidence after fix**: formatter passed; focused Telegram/outbound/cron tests passed 3 Vitest shards / 222 tests; core test typecheck passed; extensions test typecheck passed.
- **Observed result after fix**: explicit non-General forum topic sends and direct/private sends with explicit thread ids now fail closed on `message thread not found` instead of retrying threadless. General-topic fallback remains available, and async Telegram topic route preservation is covered by outbound/cron tests.
- **What was not tested**: no live Telegram Bot API/Desktop proof from a production bot after this update. Mantis visible Telegram proof was requested in the PR comments; maintainers can still require live bot proof or apply an explicit proof override.

## Verification

Current head `75acdc52684b07c8355966394f5ad5347a713e0a` was refreshed against latest `origin/main`, trimmed back to the intended nine-file effective diff, and pushed after the commands above passed locally.

## Security Impact

Reduces cross-topic message leakage in Telegram forum groups and private/direct topic contexts by failing closed when an explicit thread is stale or invalid, and by preserving topic context for automatic follow-up notices.

## Compatibility / Migration

No config migration. Non-General forum topic and direct/private topic sends now surface the Telegram error instead of silently retrying threadless. Explicit cron announce routes now persist the intended delivery context for later automatic notices.

## Risks

If an operator intentionally relied on stale explicit topic sends falling back to a root chat, those sends now fail. That behavior was the leakage bug this PR is meant to prevent.

The cron/session change is intentionally limited to resolved explicit delivery targets; bare `delivery.mode: none` jobs still avoid implicit last-target resolution.

AI-assisted refresh, simplification, validation, and PR-body update.

target.title: fix(telegram): preserve forum topic routing
target.body:
## Summary

- Keep Telegram thread-not-found fallback for General topic (`message_thread_id=1`), while failing closed for explicit non-General forum topic sends and direct/private topic sends.
- Preserve Telegram topic routing for automatic async/heartbeat follow-ups by reusing stored topic thread IDs for plugin-owned group routes.
- Seed explicit cron announce targets into isolated-session delivery context so completion and heartbeat notices stay on the configured topic instead of falling back to an agent default group.
- Keep the effective diff focused on Telegram topic routing, cron delivery context, outbound target reconstruction, and the temporary current-main memory-core test-contract shim needed for CI.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] Tests only

## Scope

Telegram extension outbound delivery, shared heartbeat target resolution, isolated cron session delivery metadata, and one test-only `memory-core` mock compatibility update for the current subagent runtime contract.

## Linked Issue / PR

- #78755 independently reported missing `message_thread_id` on outbound Telegram forum sends and was closed as duplicate/superseded by the same topic-routing work.
- #78724 covered the final session auto-reply `message_thread_id` pass-through now present on `main`; this PR keeps the broader fail-closed and async-route preservation behavior.
- Adjacent historical thread/topic work reviewed: #43808, #49704, #52824, #52286, #73900, #12293, #30892.

## Root Cause

The Telegram sender retried `400: message thread not found` by removing `message_thread_id` too broadly. That is safe only for General-topic recovery, but unsafe for explicit non-General forum topics and direct/private topic sends because Telegram can then deliver into the wrong root context.

A separate async path could reconstruct heartbeat/completion delivery from incomplete session metadata. Topic-scoped Telegram sessions and explicit cron announce jobs could lose the thread id before later automatic notices were sent.

## Regression Test Plan

- Explicit forum topic text/media/sticker/poll sends reject after the first thread-not-found error and do not retry threadless.
- Topic-qualified recipient strings such as `telegram:group:<id>:topic:<thread>` fail closed.
- Direct/private and resolved username DM sends with explicit thread ids reject after the first thread-not-found error and do not retry threadless.
- Draft stream preview/materialize sends fail closed for non-General forum topics and keep the existing stricter DM-preview behavior.
- Heartbeat target resolution preserves stored thread IDs for plugin-owned group routes when only `deliveryContext` or `origin.chatType` carries the topic context.
- Explicit Telegram cron announce targets persist session delivery context with the resolved chat id and topic thread id.

## Real Behavior Proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram topic sends must not silently retry without `message_thread_id` after Telegram reports `message thread not found`; async heartbeat/completion delivery must preserve the intended topic route instead of falling back to a group/default route.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout on NixOS Linux after refresh against current `origin/main`; branch `fix/telegram-topic-thread-fallback`; head `75acdc52684b07c8355966394f5ad5347a713e0a`.
- **Exact commands run after this patch**:

```sh
/tmp/openclaw-pr76920-merge/node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 \
  extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.test.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/bot/helpers.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/send.ts \
  src/cron/isolated-agent.direct-delivery-core-channels.test.ts \
  src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts \
  src/infra/outbound/targets.test.ts \
  src/infra/outbound/targets.ts
node scripts/test-projects.mjs \
  extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts \
  extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts \
  src/infra/outbound/targets.test.ts \
  src/cron/isolated-agent.direct-delivery-core-channels.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
```

- **Evidence after fix**: formatter passed; focused Telegram/outbound/cron tests passed 3 Vitest shards / 222 tests; core test typecheck passed; extensions test typecheck passed.
- **Observed result after fix**: explicit non-General forum topic sends and direct/private sends with explicit thread ids now fail closed on `message thread not found` instead of retrying threadless. General-topic fallback remains available, and async Telegram topic route preservation is covered by outbound/cron tests.
- **What was not tested**: no live Telegram Bot API/Desktop proof from a production bot after this update. Mantis visible Telegram proof was requested in the PR comments; maintainers can still require live bot proof or apply an explicit proof override.

## Verification

Current head `75acdc52684b07c8355966394f5ad5347a713e0a` was refreshed against latest `origin/main`, trimmed back to the intended nine-file effective diff, and pushed after the commands above passed locally.

## Security Impact

Reduces cross-topic message leakage in Telegram forum groups and private/direct topic contexts by failing closed when an explicit thread is stale or invalid, and by preserving topic context for automatic follow-up notices.

## Compatibility / Migration

No config migration. Non-General forum topic and direct/private topic sends now surface the Telegram error instead of silently retrying threadless. Explicit cron announce routes now persist the intended delivery context for later automatic notices.

## Risks

If an operator intentionally relied on stale explicit topic sends falling back to a root chat, those sends now fail. That behavior was the leakage bug this PR is meant to prevent.

The cron/session change is intentionally limited to resolved explicit delivery targets; bare `delivery.mode: none` jobs still avoid implicit last-target resolution.
